rationale: ADR-touchpoints + architectural-coherence lens. All 5 linked ADRs (0037/0035/0040/0034/0030) resolve to real files under .mindspec/adr/ — the 110 R6 spec-approve parity gate is satisfied, no dangling links. Load-bearing characterizations are accurate: ADR-0037 (R1 keeps PanelGateDecision the single decision home, changing only gathered facts; R1 actually RESTORES §4 staleness-blocks compliance for non-bead panels; R3's gate field is decision-inert per the 2026-07-09 spec-112 amendment); ADR-0035 (recovery-line convention via guard.NewFailure + five-key enumeration matches §1); ADR-0030 (R1's extra rev-parse routes through the executor seam within the per-verb subprocess budget — the spec pre-empts the very ADR the BRIEF asks about); ADR-0040 (L2/L3 layering + portability principle correctly invoked). No new architecture is smuggled: all 4 fixes are bug fixes / writer-side completions of already-decided design; next-free ADR would be 0041 and none is warranted; R3 preserves the internal/panel config-free-leaf invariant (gate resolution stays in cmd/mindspec). One non-blocking defect: the ADR-0034 touchpoint misattributes spec 112 R4's gate-enum-vs-loop.gate_authority vocabulary disambiguation to ADR-0034, which contains nothing about it. Cosmetic provenance error; link resolves, no design impact.
findings: LINK PARITY (pass): all five anchored [ADR-XXXX](../../adr/...) links resolve to existing files — ADR-0037-panel-gate-enforced-contract.md, ADR-0035-agent-error-contract.md, ADR-0040-orchestration-layering-ratchet.md, ADR-0034-ceremony-collapse.md, ADR-0030-executor-boundary.md. Satisfies the spec-110 R6 spec-approve parity gate.

ADR-0037 (R1) CORRECT: spec keeps PanelGateDecision as sole decision home and changes only the facts the CLI verbs gather. R1 in fact restores ADR-0037 §4 (staleness must Block, not Warn) for non-bead panels, which today wrongly Warn-pass via the empty bead/ ref. R3's --gate stamping is consistent with the ADR-0037 2026-07-09 (spec 112) amendment declaring `gate` decision-inert metadata never read by PanelGateDecision/ApproveThreshold.

ADR-0035 (R1/R3/R4) CORRECT: R3's invalid-gate rejection via guard.NewFailure enumerating all five keys, R1's target-named recovery lines (no bead/<empty> fragment, no `mindspec complete <bead>` advice for a non-complete-gated panel), and R4's surviving neither-set refusal all match ADR-0035 §1's recovery-line convention.

ADR-0030 (R1) CORRECT and anticipatory: the BRIEF asks whether R1's extra rev-parse should cite ADR-0030 — the spec DOES, routing through newExecutor(root).RevParseRef inside the existing one-rev-parse-per-verb budget (ADR-0037 Decision-Details also grounds the 'subprocess budget' language in ADR-0030 discipline).

ADR-0040 CORRECT: L2 (declared config) / L3 (instruct advice) and the portability principle are genuinely codified in ADR-0040; R3 as the writer-side ratchet completion and R2 as an L3-runner hardening without L2-contract change are faithful.

DEFECT (non-blocking, my lens): the ADR-0034 (Ceremony Collapse) touchpoint claims R3's five-key enum is 'deliberately distinct from loop.gate_authority's approval-act vocabulary; the rejection message preserves that disambiguation' — but ADR-0034 is about the single-bead lifecycle epic + legacy auto-migrator and contains ZERO text on gate vocabulary or gate_authority (grep-confirmed). That disambiguation actually originates in spec 112 R4 + 112's resolved OQ (bead_merge/impl_approve per 109 R4), which the spec already cites elsewhere in prose. Recommend re-attributing this touchpoint to spec 112 (or dropping the ADR-0034 link). Cosmetic provenance error only — factual content is true, link resolves, no fix-design impact — hence not a REQUEST_CHANGES.

NO NEW ADR SMUGGLED (pass): all four fixes touch no new architecture — R1/R3 are writer-side/CLI completions of the 110/112 panel-gate design, R2 a regex tightening, R4 a comment+test reconciliation. Next-free ADR number is 0041; none is needed. R3 explicitly preserves the internal/panel config-free-leaf invariant (gate-scoped resolution stays in cmd/mindspec, plain values into CreateInput.Gate) — coherent with ADR-0030's boundary and ADR-0037's single-reader leaf.
